items.title: Labor's warning over 'timing' of Mount Barker, Murray Bridge commitments

children.content: The Malinauskas government made $300 million in election promises for the cities of Mount Barker and Murray Bridge, but when they will be delivered remains uncertain after the two cities did not elect Labor MPs.

gallery.altText: A woman wearing red and a man in a suit look at a red sign which reads: Labor's plan for Murray Bridge

thumbnail.altText: A woman wearing red and a man in a suit look at a red sign which reads: Labor's plan for Murray Bridge

items.title: Teen accused of toilet block assault took 'calculated steps', court hears

children.content: Prosecutors say the allegations against a teenage boy accused of sexually assaulting a child in a toilet block are so serious he should face trial in an adult court.

image.altText: A street sign that sayd "Regano Rd", with another reading "Toilet" below it.

items.title: Butters appeal upheld, umpire abuse conviction quashed

children.content: The AFL appeals board has quashed Zak Butters' conviction for abusive language against umpire Nick Foot, saying a Tribunal member listening in while driving his car for part of the original hearing consitutes a miscarriage of justice. Follow live.

image.altText: AFL star midfielder Zak Butters.

items.title: Potato company fined $360,000 after propeller 'severely damaged' employee's arm

children.content: A Southern Mallee Potato company has been convicted and fined $360,000 after a plane propeller struck an employee's arm, leaving him with multiple injuries, including partial finger amputations.

image.altText: A crate of potato's on a farm.

items.title: Blood donation eligibility expands for gay and bisexual men, transgender people

children.content: From today, Australian Red Cross Lifeblood will use gender-neutral assessments for blood and platelets donations.

image.altText: a man sits in a reclinable chair with a bandage on his inner elbow and he squeezes a stress ball

items.title: Wine grape grower bakes cupcakes to pay bills as fuel costs bite in 'worst' harvest

children.content: Farmers in the midst of harvest in South Australia are having to get creative to help make ends meet.

gallery.altText: A fair-skinned woman in a red floral dress holds a cupcake on a glass plate in a kitchen.

thumbnail.altText: A fair-skinned woman in a red floral dress holds a cupcake on a glass plate in a kitchen.

items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate's renewed defamation claim names Australian YouTuber

children.content: An Adelaide YouTuber has been identified in a renewed defamation lawsuit self-described misogynist Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have lodged with a US court against some of their online critics.

image.altText: Andrew Tate wearing sunglasses walks with brother Tristan

items.title: Octopus species mysteriously disappears from Spencer Gulf

children.content: The once-abundant pale octopus has not been caught in lower Spencer Gulf for more than a year, and what happened to the species remains a mystery.

image.altText: An orange octopus on a blue fishing bin lip

items.title: This type of croquet is a far cry from the Alice in Wonderland version

children.content: Golf croquet is helping people reconnect with others through social competition, morning tea and laughs.

gallery.altText: Elderly woman sitting on a stool in garden holding a purple croquet mallet

thumbnail.altText: Elderly woman sitting on a stool in garden holding a purple croquet mallet

items.title: 'No rush': Crows won't put timeline on Dawson's return to play

children.content: Adelaide coach Matthew Nicks says there will be no rush for captain Jordan Dawson to return to playing after the death of his brother last week.

image.altText: The Adelaide Oval big screen displays a picture of the Dawson family with a message of support as the crowd applauds

items.title: A blunder in Narungga leaves SA's electoral authority in damage control

children.content: The 2026 state election has been a reputational disaster for South Australia's electoral authority. Its blunder in Narungga was the "cherry on top".

image.altText: A hand puts a lower house vote into a sealed ballot box as another person points to the top of the box

items.title: Driver stood down after schoolboy dragged by bus

children.content: Authorities are investigating how a 12-year-old became trapped in a bus door before being dragged hundreds of metres down a suburban street in Melbourne.

image.altText: CCTV footage of a boy dragged by a schoolbus

gallery.altText: CCTV footage of a boy dragged by a schoolbus

thumbnail.altText: CCTV footage of a boy dragged by a schoolbus

items.title: 'High-octane world' of drifting told through music in new concerto

children.content: Motorsport and orchestral music are coming together in a premiere in Adelaide this weekend.

image.altText: A clarinetist rehearsing a concerto.

items.title: Fishers deploy 'ninja tactics' to outsmart fish-stealing seals

children.content: Commercial fishers question their future as they compete with ravenous long-nosed seals that continue to rip man-sized holes in fish nets in South Australia's Coorong.

image.altText: A close up of the face of a wet seal resting on a log

items.title: 'Boy are we glad to see you': Diver describes rescue after 20 hours lost at sea

children.content: Stu Fillman was rescued alongside his dive instructor after being carried away from a dive company boat in rough swell.

gallery.altText: Mr Fillman has been left with lacerations to his neck.

thumbnail.altText: Mr Fillman has been left with lacerations to his neck.

items.title: Grieving mother of Belgian backpacker warns hikers not to trust phone maps

children.content: Backpacker Celine Cremer set off on a short walk with only a phone to guide her — and vanished. More than two years later, her family has an urgent message for other travellers.

image.altText: Ariane Mathieu, Celine Cremer's mother sits in a home space wearing a red sweater, a serious facial expression

items.title: Prospector couldn't believe his luck when he found rare australite

children.content: The object formed when a meteorite slammed into Earth more than 800,000 years ago.

image.altText: a small, round, black, slightly pitted rock held between man's fingers

items.title: The neighbourhoods where ADHD rates are skyrocketing

children.content: ADHD diagnoses in adults have exploded across Australia since 2017. For the first time, we have the data that shows where it is — and isn't — happening.

gallery.altText: A search bar saying "Enter your area" is superimposed on a map of Australia against a blue-tinged backdrop of medication.

thumbnail.altText: A search bar saying "Enter your area" is superimposed on a map of Australia against a blue-tinged backdrop of medication.

items.title: Australia's biggest AI factory planned for remote WA

children.content: A huge artificial intelligence data centre capable of training models like ChatGPT is planned for WA's remote north at a cost of billons of dollars.

image.altText: A data centre

items.title: Australia faced the same dilemma as China in 2004 but made the wrong call

children.content: While the rest of us count the cost in higher fuel costs, higher inflation and a weaker economy,  China — the actual winner of the war — will be enjoying a surge of global interest in its solar panels, wind turbines and electric vehicles.

image.altText: A man in a red hard-hat stands in a field of massive solar panels speaking to someone out of frame

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: The government is looking for cuts and thinks it has found its target

children.content: For the first time in this survey, more Australians are dissatisfied with the direction of the country than satisfied. The conflict between the US/Israel and Iran has delivered more than geopolitical uncertainty.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese wears a dark suit jacket, white shirt and red tie. He has a serious facial expression.

items.title: Quiz: Can you tame our tricky history questions?

children.content: Is two-up actually illegal in Australia? And what movie beat Crocodile Dundee to the top of the box office in 1986?

image.altText: A still of Paul Hogan holding a crocodile from the 1986 film Crocodile Dundee

items.title: Tempted to order in? Try these quick recipes instead

children.content: From frittata to one-pot pasta, these speedy dinners are perfect for busy evenings and worth adding to your regular rotation.

gallery.altText: A patterned plate of spaghetti with prawns, lemon, chilli and garlic by Julia Busuttil Nishimura. An easy spring meal.

thumbnail.altText: A patterned plate of spaghetti with prawns, lemon, chilli and garlic by Julia Busuttil Nishimura. An easy spring meal.

items.title: 'I had no-one to talk to': The 92yo trying to save his dying language

children.content: Peter is the last living speaker of the First Nations language Thiinma. When he met linguist Rosie, they used music to help keep his knowledge alive, and formed a beautiful friendship along the way.

image.altText: Peter stands on stage at a microphone with bright lights behind him, wearing an akubra, shirt and bolo tie

items.title: ASX edges higher despite rising Hormuz tension — as it happened

children.content: The ASX 200 clawed back earlier, steeper losses to close marginally higher with demand for retailers and banks outweighing selling in energy stocks and miners.

image.altText: A stock trader looks down at a tablet as he works on the floor of a busy stock exchange while surrounded by screens.

items.title: 'Hard to break the habit': Why free public transport isn't enough to shift from cars

children.content: Free public transport is a popular policy, but researchers say it's not enough to convince drivers to leave the car at home.

gallery.altText: A train on a city railway.

thumbnail.altText: A train on a city railway.

items.title: 'Privilege of my life': ACT Greens leader Shane Rattenbury quitting politics

children.content: The leader of the ACT Greens and former attorney-general Shane Rattenbury has announced he is leaving politics after more than 17 years in the Legislative Assembly.

image.altText: Shane speaks behind the podium and microphones.

items.title: Inquest opens into police employee's shooting death at hands of 15yo in 2015

children.content: A coroner will examine what authorities knew about 15-year-old gunman Farhad Jabar in the lead-up to the attack.

image.altText: a woman holding a funeral flyer

items.title: What Megan Burslem and Jeremy Fernandez can reveal about ABC Classic 100 in Concert

children.content: The ABC Classic 100 in Concert brings Australia's most popular classical music, as chosen by audiences, from the radio to the stage.

gallery.altText: Jeremy Fernandez and Megan Burslem smile and sit back to back on a piano stool in front of a grand piano on a concert stage.

thumbnail.altText: Jeremy Fernandez and Megan Burslem smile and sit back to back on a piano stool in front of a grand piano on a concert stage.

items.title: ICAC to investigate council's former boss and other staff

children.content: It comes after the City of Parramatta Council's offices were raided by ICAC in broad daylight in July as part of Operation Navarra, with little known at that time about the subject of the raid.

image.altText: A woman with red hair and glasses wearing a white shirt and black blazer.

items.title: This country music singer's tour costs have got her rethinking her career

children.content: Adele Oliver admits being a country music artist has "never been a great career choice", but she says things are worse than ever, with her last gig costing her money due to the escalating price of fuel.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair stands with a guitar strapped to her body in a home.

items.title: Two-thirds of Australians leave private information open to cybercriminals

children.content: New research suggests Australians are leaving themselves vulnerable to cybercrime through their public social media accounts.

gallery.altText: A pair of hands typing on a laptop

thumbnail.altText: A pair of hands typing on a laptop

items.title: RSL denies services club's Anzac Day two-up request

children.content: An ex-services organisation wants changes to Queensland legislation that allows the local RSL branch to reject its application to play two-up on Anzac Day.

image.altText: An elderly hand holds a small woodern plank with two copper coins on it

items.title: How free is netball's new TV deal?

children.content: Uncertainties over the details of Netball Australia's broadcast agreement with Nine left fans hesitant to celebrate. Now we reveal how many games will actually be seen on free-to-air TV.

image.altText: Four women stand holding netballs in front of Sydney Harbour

items.title: Red-hot golfer Green blazes to third LA Championship triumph

children.content: Australian golf ace Hannah Green wins her fourth title of the season, taking out the LPGA Tour's LA Championship for a third time in four years.

gallery.altText: An Australian golfer smiles as she stands on a green holding a large trophy after winning an LPGA tournament.

thumbnail.altText: An Australian golfer smiles as she stands on a green holding a large trophy after winning an LPGA tournament.
